name: GraphHopper Routing and Optimization API
what_it_does: Open-source routing engine with commercial API — enabling developers and logistics companies to calculate routes, perform route optimization (Vehicle Routing Problem), geocode addresses, compute isochrones, and build navigation applications, powered by OpenStreetMap data with custom routing profiles and EU data residency.
use_cases: Calculating optimal routes via GraphHopper API for delivery fleet navigation and planning
Solving Vehicle Routing Problems via GraphHopper Optimization API for last-mile logistics
Geocoding and reverse geocoding addresses via GraphHopper API for location-based services
Computing isochrones via GraphHopper API for travel time analysis and service area planning
Building navigation applications via GraphHopper self-hosted for custom routing requirements
not_for: Turn-by-turn navigation apps needing real-time traffic data (GraphHopper is OSM-based, not real-time traffic)
Teams needing Google Maps-quality map tiles (GraphHopper is routing/geocoding, not map display)
Real-time ETA prediction requiring live traffic feeds
best_when: A logistics, delivery, or mobility team needs GDPR-compliant, EU-hosted routing with advanced VRP optimization — at competitive pricing using OpenStreetMap data, with optional self-hosted deployment.
avoid_when: You need real-time traffic integration, map tile rendering, or Google Maps ecosystem compatibility.

auth.notes: API key via query parameter or header (key=). No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. No webhooks. JavaScript and Java SDKs. Self-hosted open source (Apache 2.0) for full data control. Commercial API for managed routing. Async job API for large optimization problems (webhook-like callback). OpenStreetMap data — no proprietary data dependency.

pricing.paid_tiers: Startup €99/month; Business €399/month; Enterprise custom; Self-hosted free (open source)
pricing.notes: German company (Berlin). Founded 2012 by Peter Karich and Michael Zilske. Bootstrapped and profitable. Apache 2.0 open source core. GitHub: graphhopper/graphhopper (5,000+ stars). Written in Java. OpenStreetMap data basis. GDPR-compliant EU data residency. Strong in European logistics and fleet management. Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) optimization as key feature. Route Optimization API for multi-stop delivery planning. Matrix API for origin-destination travel times. Used by: delivery companies, fleet managers, mobility apps.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with API key via parameter or header. Good documentation. JS and Java SDKs. No webhooks. GDPR compliant. German hosting. Free tier 500 requests/day. Open source self-hosted option. VRP optimization is async job — poll for completion. OpenStreetMap data basis — verify OSM coverage for your region.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Routing requests fully idempotent for same waypoints. Geocoding idempotent for same query. Isochrone idempotent for same parameters. Optimization jobs idempotent by request parameters.
known_agent_gotchas: VRP optimization is async (jobs) — submit job, poll for result; not real-time
API key via query parameter — ensure HTTPS only to protect key in URL
OpenStreetMap data — accuracy depends on OSM community contribution in the area
500 requests/day on free tier — insufficient for production routing apps
No real-time traffic — routes optimized for typical conditions, not live traffic

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. German hosting (EU data residency). API key via query parameter — use HTTPS exclusively. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. OpenStreetMap data — no personal user data in routing requests. Self-hosted option provides full data sovereignty. Open source Apache 2.0 — auditable Java code. Route data (waypoints) may contain PII — handle with GDPR care.
